title: Egyptian Herbal Gum Arabic Dough Flatbread
description: A unique and aromatic flatbread from Egypt, infused with herbal flavors and topped with a tangy sour cream.

ingredients.name: All-purpose Flour
ingredients.notes: use a gluten-free flour blend for a gluten-free version

instructions: In a small bowl, combine warm water, yeast, and 1 teaspoon of sugar. Let it sit for 5-7 minutes until frothy.
In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, black sesame seeds, dried mint leaves, and dried rose petals.
Add the yeast mixture to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 2-3 equal pieces.
Shape each piece into a ball and then flatten it into a disk.
Transfer the flatbreads to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the olive oil over the flatbreads and sprinkle with black sesame seeds.
Bake the flatbreads in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes until lightly browned.
While the flatbreads are baking, mix the sour cream and lemon juice in a bowl.
Once the flatbreads are done, remove them from the oven and let them cool for 2-3 minutes.
Top each flatbread with a dollop of sour cream mixture and serve.
